---
title: "Cheatsheet · Discovery algorithms"
subtitle: "Learning structure from data"
---

::: {.cheatsheet}

## The families

| Algorithm | Family | Idea | Watch out |
|---|---|---|---|
| **PC** | constraint | conditional-independence tests → skeleton → orient v-structures | leaves edges undirected; test errors propagate |
| **FCI** | constraint | like PC but allows latent confounders | slower; outputs a PAG |
| **GES** | score | greedily maximize BIC over equivalence classes | robust to single test errors |
| **LiNGAM** | functional | linear + non-Gaussian ⇒ a unique DAG | strong distributional assumption |
| **NOTEARS** | continuous | differentiable acyclicity constraint | scalable; more spurious edges |

## Output: a CPDAG, not a DAG

Constraint- and score-based methods return an **equivalence class**. Some edges
stay undirected because the data cannot orient them. Report the class, not a
single graph.

## Constraints (domain knowledge)

[Forbidden edges.]{.lead-in} Exclude impossible relations. &nbsp;
[Required edges.]{.lead-in} Force known relations. &nbsp;
[Temporal tiers.]{.lead-in} Causes must precede effects.

## Scoring a recovered graph

| Metric | Question |
|---|---|
| Precision | of discovered edges, how many are correct? |
| Recall | of true edges, how many were found? |
| F1 (directed) | harmonic mean, direction matters |
| F1 (skeleton) | same, ignoring direction |

**Edge classification:** correct · reversed · spurious · missed.

::: {.pullquote}
Algorithms disagree — on one benchmark PC and GES agree on only ~20% of edges.
That disagreement feeds the [complexity score](../workflow/02-complexity-score.qmd).
:::

## Python, today

`causal-learn` (PC / GES / LiNGAM, pure-Python-friendly) and `gcastle` (NOTEARS +
gradient methods) are pip-installable. This project ships PC and GES **live**;
LiNGAM and NOTEARS appear as precomputed appendix columns.

:::

*Print for a handout.* Run these live in the [app](../app.qmd).
